$comment: Protocol coverage manifest (Inc 1 of docs/design/protocol-contract.md). Every schema exported by the vendored gateway-protocol modules (protocol/openclaw/<version>/) must be classified here — either whole-schema or field-by-field. The ratchet test (test/protocol-coverage.test.ts) walks the vendored TypeBox schemas and fails on any unclassified schema/field, so a validated-version bump that introduces new protocol surface stays RED until a human classifies it. statuses: handled (consumed/emitted by the bridge, `by` = where) | ignored (deliberate, `why` required) | gap (known-unsupported, `note` required). Seeded from the audit in docs/design/protocol-schema-coverage.md.

agentId.why: the connection is already session-scoped per agent (buildSessionKey)

seq.by: handleChat dedup key component; NOTE: no ordering/gap detection (ordered WS transport)

replace.note: a bare-deltaText replacement delta (replace:true, no message) APPENDS instead of replacing — corrupts streamed text; message-carrying replacements are covered incidentally by the snapshot path. No fixture observed yet.

usage.note: main-turn token/cost telemetry absent (child telemetry reads payload.session.*, not usage)

message.why: an aborted turn carries no reply to persist

errorKind.by: handleChat -> finalize errorKind -> messages.errorCode + gateway_pressure trace (context_length = hard overflow)

seq.by: frame dedup/tally; no gap detection (ordered WS)

ts.why: the normalizer runs on an injected monotonic clock; frame timestamps unused

isHeartbeat.why: keep-alive; any own frame already refreshes the recv budget (armRecv)

AgentInternalEvent.why: the FORMAL announce contract (task_completion: childSessionKey/status/result/attachments) — but these are run INPUT params (AgentParams.internalEvents), invisible on the event stream by construction; the bridge reconstructs announce deliveries from the announce:v1 run's normal chat/assistant frames. Future structured source if the gateway ever surfaces them.

AgentGeneratedAttachment.why: carried inside AgentInternalEvent (run input params) — see AgentInternalEvent

agentId.why: redundant with the agent baked into sessionKey

sessionId.why: the gateway resolves the session from sessionKey

thinking.why: reasoning level is set via sessions.patch (knob), not per-send

fastMode.why: fast mode is set via sessions.patch (knob), not per-send

fastAutoOnSeconds.why: auto fast-mode cutoff not exposed by Atrium

deliver.why: Atrium consumes the event stream; channel delivery unused

originatingChannel.why: no channel-mirroring semantics in Atrium sends

timeoutMs.why: turn timeouts are owned by the normalizer's recv deadlines

systemInputProvenance.why: operator-injection provenance not applicable to user sends

suppressCommandInterpretation.why: Atrium sends are user turns; command interpretation stays default

ChatAbortParams.note: chat.abort is NEVER called: Atrium's stop button ends the turn client/Convex-side but the gateway run keeps executing (audit finding). Wiring it = a real cancel.

ChatInjectParams.why: operator-visible synthetic transcript injection — no Atrium feature maps to it

ChatHistoryParams.why: history recovery uses sessions.get (bounded, keyed) instead of chat.history

ChatMetadataParams.why: chat metadata listing unused; agent discovery goes through agents.list

ChatMessageGetParams.why: single-message lookup unused (Convex is the message store)

ChatMessageGetResult.why: result envelope of the unused chat.message.get

ChatEvent.why: the union wrapper of the four chat event schemas classified field-by-field above

LogsTailParams.why: dashboard log tailing — not an Atrium surface (SOC2: no raw gateway logs in-product)

MessageActionToolContext.why: channel message-action tooling — Atrium is not a channel adapter

MessageActionParams.why: channel message actions — not an Atrium surface

SendParams.why: outbound channel sends (Telegram-style) — Atrium consumes the event stream instead

PollParams.why: native channel polls — not an Atrium surface

AgentParams.why: the direct agent-run RPC — the bridge dispatches turns via chat.send exclusively (session-keyed, idempotent)

AgentIdentityParams.why: identity display comes from agents.list at discovery time

AgentWaitParams.why: run completion is observed on the event stream, never polled

WakeParams.why: external scheduler wake — not an Atrium surface

InputProvenance.why: operator-injection provenance primitive — Atrium sends never set systemInputProvenance (see ChatSendParams)

GatewayClientId.why: handshake client-id vocabulary — the bridge identifies via its own connect params, not this enum

GatewayClientMode.why: handshake client-mode vocabulary — same as GatewayClientId

SecretRefSource.why: secret-reference primitive for config schemas — the bridge never writes gateway config

SecretRef.why: secret-reference primitive — the bridge never writes gateway config

SecretInput.why: secret-input primitive — the bridge never writes gateway config
